items.text: Moreover, because of various concerns jurisdictions may have with respect to our business, including any decisions we may make relating to entering or exiting a 28 MASTERCARD 2025 FORM 10-K PART I ITEM 1A.
items.rationale: This is a page header fragment merged with introductory text, not a substantive risk statement.

items.text: With respect to information security, any single breach could require parallel notifications to data protection authorities, cyber authorities and/or law-enforcement, often requiring different thresholds, reporting deadlines and formats.
items.rationale: Adds specific detail about the complexity of breach notification requirements to an already-existing information security risk.

items.text: Participants may also withhold rights to data we use to power our solutions in order to support their own potential future solutions, potentially impacting the effectiveness of our solutions.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific new risk that participants may withhold data rights, impacting the effectiveness of the company's solutions.

items.text: In addition, the current or future listing of Recorded Future as an “undesirable” or “unreliable” entity by certain jurisdictions could further increase our risks in this area.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific new risk regarding Recorded Future being listed as an 'undesirable' or 'unreliable' entity by certain jurisdictions.

items.text: We switch a high percentage of domestic (or in-country) transactions conducted using cards with our brands.
items.rationale: Describes an existing core business characteristic (switching domestic transactions) without introducing new risk detail.

items.text: However, there are several jurisdictions in which domestic transactions are switched by our customers or other processors.
items.rationale: Factual clarifying statement about existing business operations, not a new or expanded risk.

items.text: Additionally, we or our customers could take (or be perceived to take) actions related to these industries, which could be viewed negatively and result in threats or other retaliatory actions.
items.rationale: Introduces specific new risk of retaliatory actions resulting from perceived actions related to certain industries.
Page header/footer text, not risk content.
Page header/footer text, not risk content.

items.text: In addition, Brazil recently enacted regulation requiring PSOs in Brazil (including Mastercard and Visa) to extend their responsibility for the financial and settlement integrity of payments to merchants.
items.rationale: Introduces specific new regulatory risk from Brazil requiring PSOs to extend responsibility for financial and settlement integrity.

items.text: As we continue to be subject to increased regulation across the globe, more jurisdictions may enact similar approaches from time to time.
items.rationale: Generalizes and expands the existing regulatory risk to suggest broader adoption of similar approaches across jurisdictions.

items.text: Any such changes could increase complexity and may impact our cost of operations and financial condition.
items.rationale: Generic concluding statement about complexity and cost impact without new specifics.
Page header/footer text, not risk content.
